memory.summary: My friends are pushing hard for me to join their night out, but my midterm tomorrow and current fatigue make staying in a much better long-term investment.
Pulling out tonight protects my sleep and study time, which are far more valuable to our group's long-term success than this single night.

I've made the hard choice to prioritize our group's long-term success over tonight's fun, trusting that my presence tomorrow will benefit everyone more than this single night out.
By leaving, I've reinforced my commitment to our group's long-term success over tonight's fun. This sacrifice protects my ability to contribute fully tomorrow, which matters more than one night out.

goals.rationale: My Affiliation value (0.5) dictates that maintaining trust with cohort_3 (closeness 7) is paramount. The current cutoff 'I v...' looks like an abrupt rejection of *us*. I need to finish this thought immediately to prevent resentment before I can explain the long-term necessity of sacrificing tonight's fun for tomorrow's performance.

goals.description: Execute a strict sleep protocol: put away all screens 45 minutes from now and be in bed by 23:30 to ensure 8 hours of rest before the Fri 09:00 exam.
goals.rationale: While my Assertiveness axis tells me to set boundaries on social demands, my Deliberate time perspective and Health priority (0.13) require me to invest in future payoff. Studying now will only lead to mental fog during the midterm. Protecting my energy is a strategic move to ensure I can be reliable for both the exam and the group moving forward.
